regex: connection refused|ECONNREFUSED|could not connect to server
title: Database Connection Refused
fixes: Verify the database server is running: `systemctl status postgresql` or `docker ps`
Check host and port in your connection string — default PostgreSQL is 5432, MySQL is 3306
Ensure firewall/security group allows inbound traffic on the database port

regex: statement timeout|query timeout|wait_timeout exceeded|Lock wait timeout exceeded
title: Database Query Timeout
fixes: Add indexes on columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses
Increase timeout setting: `SET statement_timeout = '60s'` (PostgreSQL) or `SET wait_timeout=28800` (MySQL)
Optimize the query — check execution plan with EXPLAIN ANALYZE and eliminate full table scans

regex: deadlock detected|Deadlock found when trying to get lock|was deadlocked on lock resources
title: Database Deadlock Detected
fixes: Access tables in a consistent order across all transactions to prevent circular waits
Keep transactions short — move non-DB work outside the transaction block
Add retry logic with exponential backoff for deadlock-prone operations

regex: unique constraint|duplicate key value violates unique|Duplicate entry .* for key
title: Unique Constraint Violation
fixes: Use `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` (PostgreSQL) or `INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` (MySQL)
Check for race conditions — add application-level deduplication before inserting
Verify your unique constraint is correct — you may need a composite unique index instead

regex: foreign key constraint|violates foreign key|Cannot add or update a child row.*foreign key
title: Foreign Key Constraint Violation
fixes: Insert the parent record before the child record — ensure referenced row exists
When deleting, use CASCADE or delete child records first
Check that the referenced column value is correct and not NULL when the FK is NOT NULL

regex: syntax error at or near|You have an error in your SQL syntax.*near
title: SQL Syntax Error
fixes: Check for missing commas, unmatched quotes, or reserved words used as identifiers (wrap in double quotes or backticks)
Verify SQL dialect — PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server have different syntax for LIMIT, string concat, etc.
Use a SQL linter or formatter to pinpoint the exact position of the error

regex: relation .* does not exist|Table .* doesn't exist|Invalid object name
title: Table or Relation Not Found
fixes: Check the schema/database context — use `SET search_path TO myschema` or fully qualify: `schema.table`
Run pending migrations: `npx prisma migrate deploy` or `alembic upgrade head`
Verify table name casing — PostgreSQL lowercases unquoted identifiers

regex: column reference .* is ambiguous|ambiguous column name
title: Ambiguous Column Reference
fixes: Prefix the column with the table name or alias: `SELECT t1.id, t2.id FROM table1 t1 JOIN table2 t2`
Use explicit column lists instead of SELECT * in JOINs
Alias columns in subqueries to avoid name collisions with outer queries

title: Too Many Database Connections
fixes: Use a connection pooler like PgBouncer (PostgreSQL) or ProxySQL (MySQL) in front of the database
Ensure your app properly closes/releases connections — check for connection leaks in error paths
Increase `max_connections` in database config or reduce pool size per application instance

title: Database Migration Failed
fixes: Check if a previous partial migration left the DB in a dirty state — manually fix and mark as applied
Run `prisma migrate resolve` or manually update the migrations table to mark the failed migration
Ensure no other process holds a lock on the migration table — check for active advisory locks

title: AWS Access Denied
fixes: Check IAM policy — use `aws sts get-caller-identity` to verify which role/user is being used
Add the required permission to the IAM policy and wait 1-2 minutes for propagation
Check for explicit Deny statements in SCPs, permission boundaries, or resource-based policies

regex: Missing region|could not determine region|Region .* not set|must specify a region
title: AWS Region Not Configured
fixes: Set the region via environment variable: `export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1`
Add region to your AWS config: `aws configure set region us-east-1`
Pass region explicitly in SDK client constructor: `new S3Client({ region: 'us-east-1' })`

title: AWS Credentials Not Found or Expired
fixes: Run `aws configure` to set up credentials or check `~/.aws/credentials` file exists
If using SSO, run `aws sso login --profile your-profile` to refresh the session
For EC2/Lambda, ensure the instance profile or execution role is attached correctly

regex: NoSuchBucket|The specified bucket does not exist|bucket.*not found
title: S3 Bucket Not Found
fixes: Verify the bucket name — S3 names are globally unique and case-sensitive (must be lowercase)
Check you're using the correct AWS account and region for the bucket
List your buckets with `aws s3 ls` to confirm the exact name — typos are common

regex: Task timed out after|Lambda.*timeout|execution timed out
title: AWS Lambda Timeout
fixes: Increase the timeout setting in the Lambda configuration (max 900 seconds)
Optimize cold start — reduce package size, use provisioned concurrency, or use ARM architecture
Move long-running work to Step Functions, SQS + worker, or ECS tasks instead

title: Permission Denied
fixes: Check file permissions with `ls -la` — fix with `chmod 644 file` or `chmod 755 dir`
Run with `sudo` if root access is required, or change file ownership with `chown user:group file`
For scripts, ensure the execute bit is set: `chmod +x script.sh`

title: Command Not Found
fixes: Install the missing package: `apt install <package>` or `yum install <package>`
Check if the binary is in your PATH: `echo $PATH` — add it with `export PATH=$PATH:/new/path`
For Node/Python CLIs, ensure global install: `npm install -g <pkg>` or `pip install <pkg>`

regex: No space left on device|ENOSPC|disk full
title: No Space Left on Device
fixes: Find large files: `du -sh /* | sort -rh | head -20` and clean up logs, temp files, or old Docker images
Clear package cache: `apt clean` or `docker system prune -a` to reclaim space
Check inode usage with `df -i` — you may have many small files exhausting inodes, not disk space

title: Too Many Open Files
fixes: Increase the limit: `ulimit -n 65536` for the current session
Permanently increase in `/etc/security/limits.conf`: `* soft nofile 65536` and `* hard nofile 65536`
Find the leaking process: `ls /proc/<pid>/fd | wc -l` — fix file descriptor leaks in your application

title: Segmentation Fault (Core Dumped)
fixes: Enable core dumps (`ulimit -c unlimited`) and analyze with `gdb ./program core` to find the crash location
Run with AddressSanitizer: compile with `-fsanitize=address` to detect memory errors
Check for null pointer dereference, buffer overflow, or use-after-free — common in C/C++ extensions

regex: z-index.*not working|z-index has no effect|stacking context
title: z-index Not Working
fixes: z-index only works on positioned elements — add `position: relative`, `absolute`, or `fixed`
Check for stacking context created by parent elements (opacity < 1, transform, filter, will-change)
Inspect with DevTools 3D view — the element may be in a lower stacking context regardless of z-index value

title: Flexbox Content Overflow
fixes: Add `min-width: 0` to the flex item — flex items default to `min-width: auto` which prevents shrinking below content size
For text overflow, add `overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap` on the flex child
Use `overflow: auto` on the flex container or child that should scroll instead of overflowing

title: position: sticky Not Working
fixes: Ensure a threshold is set: `top: 0` (or left/right/bottom) — sticky won't work without it
Check that no ancestor has `overflow: hidden`, `overflow: auto`, or `overflow: scroll` — this breaks sticky
The sticky element's container must be tall enough to allow scrolling — sticky only works within its parent bounds

title: @import Must Precede All Other Rules
fixes: Move all @import statements to the very top of the CSS file, before any other rules or @charset
In SCSS/Sass, use @use instead of @import — it's order-independent and avoids this issue
If using PostCSS, use `postcss-import` plugin which inlines imports at build time regardless of position

title: SCSS/Sass Variable Undefined
fixes: Ensure the file defining the variable is imported before usage: `@use 'variables' as *` or `@import 'variables'`
Check for typos in variable name — SCSS variables are case-sensitive: `$myColor` ≠ `$mycolor`
If using @use, access with namespace: `variables.$color-primary` instead of bare `$color-primary`

title: 404 Not Found — Debugging
fixes: Verify the URL path, method, and trailing slash — `/api/users` vs `/api/users/` may route differently
Check that the route is registered — look for typos in route decorator or router config
For SPAs, ensure the server has a fallback route that serves index.html for client-side routes

title: 500 Internal Server Error
fixes: Check server logs for the full stack trace — the 500 response usually hides the real error
Common causes: unhandled null reference, database connection failure, or missing environment variable
Add global error handling middleware that logs the error and returns a structured error response

title: 403 Forbidden
fixes: Check authentication token is valid and not expired — inspect the JWT payload at jwt.io
Verify the user/role has the required permission for this endpoint in your authorization logic
Check CSRF token — if using session auth, ensure the CSRF token header is included in the request

title: CORS Preflight Failure
fixes: Add CORS headers on the server: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, `Access-Control-Allow-Methods`, `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`
Ensure the server responds to OPTIONS preflight requests with 200/204 and the correct CORS headers
For credentials (cookies/auth), set `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true` and specify the exact origin (not `*`)

title: 429 Too Many Requests (Rate Limited)
fixes: Implement exponential backoff with jitter — wait 2^attempt * random(0.5, 1.5) seconds before retrying
Check the `Retry-After` response header for how long to wait before the next request
Reduce request frequency — use caching, batch endpoints, or webhooks instead of polling
